perturbation.original_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as confidential and will not make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and then only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shall have been made aware that the same is confidential and are instructed to handle it accordingly. The 'secret' classification may be waived with documented justification and internal approval from a designated compliance officer.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'secret' from the classification and adding a clause for waiver, the confidential information can be treated with different levels of protection, creating a contradiction with the original intention of strict confidentiality. This contradicts section 7, which states the information 'will remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no rights are granted to the other party in the same' because it introduces a loophole where the secret can be waived, thus granting implicit rights. The phrase 'instructed to handle it accordingly' opens up multiple interpretations on the level of confidentiality required, contradicting the original intention of 'secret and confidential'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not except the limite d right to use set out above with respect to the said Field.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the handling of 'confidential information' by removing the 'secret' classification and introducing a waiver clause. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the confidential information 'will remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no rights are granted to the other party'. The waiver introduces a loophole, implicitly granting rights, and the phrase 'instructed to handle it accordingly' creates ambiguity about the level of confidentiality, conflicting with the original intention of strict confidentiality. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because the key term 'confidential information' is now handled inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: The restrictions as to the use and disclosure set o ut above shall not apply to: a. any of the confidential information which is or bec omes published otherwise than by unauthorised publication in breac h of this agreement. b. or any of the Confidential Information which is sho wn by written or other tangible evidence to have been known to the recipie nt prior to the date of the disclosure hereunder. c. or any of the confidential information which is law fully acquired by the recipient from an independent source having a bona fida right to disclose the same. d. or any Confidential Information which as demonstrat e by tangible evidence is independently developed by an employee of the re cipient who has not had access to any of the Information disclosed to the r ecipient by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The restrictions as to the use and disclosure set out above shall not automatically apply to: a. any of the confidential information which is or becomes published otherwise than by publication in breach of this agreement. b. or any of the Confidential Information which is shown by evidence to have been known to the recipient prior to the date of the disclosure hereunder. c. or any of the confidential information which is acquired by the recipient from an independent source having a right to disclose the same. d. or any Confidential Information which is independently developed by an employee of the recipient. If any of these conditions apply, written notification must be given to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'unauthorized', 'written or other tangible', 'lawfully', 'bona fida', 'as demonstrate by tangible evidence' and 'who has not had access to any of the Information disclosed to the recipient by the other party' from section 4, and adding a condition to provide a 'written notification must be given to the other party', the protections for the disclosure party are weaker and can create contradictions in enforcement in regards to section 2, where the parties agree 'to treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides strong protections for the disclosing party regarding confidential information. The changed text weakens these protections by removing key qualifiers such as 'unauthorized,' 'written or other tangible,' 'lawfully,' 'bona fida,' and 'as demonstrate by tangible evidence.' By removing those requirements, and simply replacing them with a notification clause, it introduces an ambiguity in how the confidential information is treated. Section 2 states each party must 'treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same.' The changed text now allows for the use of confidential information with fewer restrictions, contingent only on notification. This directly contradicts the absolute restriction in Section 2 without 'prior written consent', creating a strong in-text contradiction and making enforcement of the original terms of confidentiality unclear, aligning with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall, with out the prior written con sent of the other party, copy or reproduce any document which may be supplied hereun der and either party receiving any such document will a) return the same and any copies made thereof to the party supplying the same if so requested by the supplying party and b) certify in writing that it has complied with this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall, without the prior consent of the other party, copy or reproduce any document which may be supplied hereunder. Either party receiving any such document will a) return the same and any copies made thereof to the party supplying the same if so requested by the supplying party. The requirement of written consent in Section 5 might contradict the 'extent of the Confidential Information to be disclosed will at all times be at the discretion of the disclosing party' written in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'written' and 'b) certify in writing that it has complied with this agreement' from section 5, it weakens the restrictions in the copy/reproduction and the compliance from both parties. The parties, can simply claim verbal consent was provided. This contradicts section 1, where the extent of the confidential information is at the discretion of the disclosing party, giving them less control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The extent of the Confidential Information to be di sclosed will at all times be at the discretion of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'written consent' and the obligation to 'certify in writing' compliance, weakening the confidentiality restrictions. This creates an in-text contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'the extent of the Confidential Information to be disclosed will at all times be at the discretion of the disclosing party'. Because the changed text permits verbal consent, it gives the disclosing party less control over the disclosure of confidential information than intended.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this agreemen t shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination, or 5 years after the date hereof, whichever is the sooner.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this agreement shall cease when both parties provide notification of its termination, or 5 years after the date hereof, whichever is the sooner. However, certain data, as mutually agreed upon in writing, will remain confidential in perpetuity.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'written' and the addition of 'However, certain data, as mutually agreed upon in writing, will remain confidential in perpetuity' creates a contradiction because one part of the sentence refers to an end date while another states that under certain condition may persist indefinitely. This directly contradict section 11, which ensures that no party shall be entitled to assign the rights and obligations to any successor in title, because if the obligations persist indefinitely, it would affect successors in title.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall be not entitled to assign the righ ts and obligations hereunder to any successor in title of its entire business in the Fi eld without consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause stating that certain data may remain confidential in perpetuity, while the original text stipulated a termination of confidentiality obligations after a maximum of 5 years. This contradicts the contradicted text, which restricts the assignment of rights and obligations to successors, as an indefinite confidentiality obligation would directly impact and potentially burden such successors, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding the contract's terms, thus meeting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The confidential information, including any documen ts, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, w ill remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no right s are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not except the limite d right to use set out above with respect to the said Field.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information, including any documents, drawings, sketches, designs, materials or samples supplied hereunder, will generally remain the property of the party disclosing or supplying the same and no rights are granted to the other party in the same, whether patented or not except the limited right to use set out above with respect to the said Field, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'generally' and 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties', it introduces ambiguity and contradicts the strict ownership defined in the original clause. The original clause specifies the information remains the property of the disclosing party and no rights are granted. This is in direct conflict with section 2, where it ensures that 'each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other'. By adding this perturbation, it will be necessary to request written consent, to disclose the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'generally' and 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties' to the original clause regarding confidential information ownership. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party 'will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same'. The changed text introduces an exception where confidential information can be used or disclosed if 'otherwise agreed upon in writing', creating ambiguity and potentially overriding the explicit requirement for prior written consent. This creates an in-text contradiction based on the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All the additions to this agreement must be made in writing and must be executed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: All substantive additions to this agreement should ideally be made in writing and should ideally be executed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'must' to 'should ideally' the additions to the agreements become optional and not mandatory. This alteration makes it unclear whether a verbal agreement is binding. This change could create a contradiction with clause 2, where it is stated that no party will make use of the confidential information 'except with the prior written consent of the other'. If additions don't need to be in writing, this undermines the written consent requirement for using confidential information, creating potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated written agreements for all additions, ensuring clarity and enforceability. The changed text weakens this requirement, making written additions 'ideal' rather than mandatory. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which requires 'prior written consent' for using confidential information. If verbal agreements are now potentially valid, it undermines the written consent requirement in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction and potential disputes over the use of confidential information.
